 36→/**
 37→ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 38→ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 39→ *
 40→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 41→ *
 42→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 43→ */
 44→public class AnnotationRule : StandardRule("annotation") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        when (node.elementType) {
 51→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 52→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→            ANNOTATION -> {
 55→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 56→            }
 57→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 58→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 59→        }
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63→        node: ASTNode,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66→    ) {
 67→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68→
 69→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72→            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74→        ) {
 75→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80→        ) {
 81→            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86→            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87→        ) {
 88→            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→        }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96→    ) {
 97→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                node.startOffset,
100→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                node
105→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107→            }
108→        }
109→
110→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111→            emit(
112→                node.startOffset,
113→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119→            )
120→            if (autoCorrect) {
121→                node
122→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
123→                    .nextLeaf()
124→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
125→                    ?.let {
126→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
127→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
128→                        } else {
129→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131→                            )
132→                        }
133→                    }
134→            }
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139→        node: ASTNode,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142→    ) {
143→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144→            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
145→                node
146→                    .siblings(forward = false)
147→                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
148→            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149→                emit(
150→                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
151→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
152→                    true,
153→                )
154→                if (autoCorrect) {
155→                    node
156→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
157→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
158→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
159→                }
160→            }
161→        }
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ) {
169→        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170→            node
171→                .siblings(forward = true)
172→                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173→        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
174→            node
175→                .siblings(forward = false)
176→                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
177→        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178→            emit(
179→                node.startOffset,
180→                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181→                true,
182→            )
183→            if (autoCorrect) {
184→                node
185→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .nextLeaf()
187→                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
193→
194→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
195→        psi
196→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
197→            ?.useSiteTarget
198→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
199→
200→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
203→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
204→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
207→        treeParent
208→            .children()
209→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
210→            .let { it == this }
211→
212→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
213→
214→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
215→        siblings(forward = false)
216→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
217→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
218→
219→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
220→
221→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
222→        siblings(forward = true)
223→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
224→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
225→
226→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
227→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→            .leaves(forward = true)
229→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
230→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
231→
232→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
233→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
234→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
235→        } else {
236→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
237→        }
238→
239→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240→        node: ASTNode,
241→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243→    ) {
244→        node
245→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246→            .nextCodeLeaf()
247→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249→
250→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251→                    emit(
252→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254→                        true,
255→                    )
256→                    if (autoCorrect) {
257→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
258→                    }
259→                }
260→            }
261→    }
262→
263→    private fun visitAnnotation(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→    ) {
268→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269→
270→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272→        ) {
273→            emit(
274→                node.startOffset,
275→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
276→                true,
277→            )
278→            if (autoCorrect) {
279→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
280→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
282→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283→                }
284→            }
285→        }
286→    }
287→
288→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290→        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291→        // text in the file
292→        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293→        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294→            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295→            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296→        } else {
297→            newLineWithIndent
298→        }
299→    }
300→}
301→
302→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId

 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 22→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtContainerNode
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDoWhileExpression
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtIfExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhileExpression

 31→/**
 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
202→                    }
203→                }
204→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
205→            }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
216→        }
217→
218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->
220→            val elementType = child.elementType
221→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
223→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
228→        treeParent.children()
229→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}
266→
267→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId

311→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315→    ) {
316→        for (c in node.children()) {
317→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320→                else -> false
321→            }
322→            if (hasLineBreak) {
323→                // rearrange
324→                //
325→                // a, b, value(
326→                // ), c, d
327→                //
328→                // to
329→                //
330→                // a, b,
331→                // value(
332→                // ),
333→                // c, d
334→
335→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337→                if (
338→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→                ) {
341→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342→                }
343→                // insert \n after multi-line value
344→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347→                if (
348→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351→                    // value(
352→                    // ), // a comment
353→                    // c, d
354→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355→                ) {
356→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357→                }
358→            }
359→        }
360→    }

107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }

62→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63→        node: ASTNode,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66→    ) {
 67→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68→
 69→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72→            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74→        ) {
 75→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80→        ) {
 81→            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86→            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87→        ) {
 88→            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→        }
 90→    }

 92→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96→    ) {
 97→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                node.startOffset,

311→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315→    ) {

318→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320→                else -> false
321→            }
322→            if (hasLineBreak) {
323→                // rearrange
324→                //
325→                // a, b, value(
326→                // ), c, d
327→                //
328→                // to
329→                //
330→                // a, b,
331→                // value(
332→                // ),
333→                // c, d
334→
335→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337→                if (
338→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→                ) {
341→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342→                }
343→                // insert \n after multi-line value
344→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347→                if (
348→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351→                    // value(
352→                    // ), // a comment
353→                    // c, d
354→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355→                ) {
356→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357→                }
358→            }
359→        }
360→    }

 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    StandardRule(
 87→        id = "wrapping",
 88→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 89→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92→        ),
 93→    ) {
 94→    private var line = 1
 95→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        line = 1
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129→
130→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132→            return
133→        }
134→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137→        val wrapBlock =
138→            when {
139→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143→                }
144→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
145→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf {
146→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
147→                    }
148→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf {
149→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
150→                    }
151→                    val line =
152→                        startOfLine
153→                            ?.leaves()
154→                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
155→                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
156→                            .orEmpty()
157→                    line.length > maxLineLength
158→                }
159→                else -> false
160→            }
161→        if (wrapBlock) {
162→            startOfBlock
163→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
164→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
165→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
166→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
167→                        autoCorrect,
168→                        emit,
169→                    )
170→                }
171→        }
172→    }
173→
174→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
175→        node: ASTNode,
176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
178→    ) {
179→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
180→        var newlineInBetween = false
181→        var parameterListInBetween = false
182→        var numberOfArgs = 0
183→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
184→        // matching ), ] or }
185→        val r = node.nextSibling {
186→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
187→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
188→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
189→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
190→            if (isValueArgument) {
191→                numberOfArgs++
192→                firstArg = it
193→            }
194→            it.elementType == rElementType
195→        }!!
196→        if (
197→            !newlineInBetween ||
198→            // keep { p ->
199→            // }
200→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
201→            // keep ({
202→            // }) and (object : C {
203→            // })
204→            (
205→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
206→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
207→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
208→                )
209→        ) {
210→            return
211→        }
212→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
213→            // keep:
214→            // for (foo in listOf(
215→            //     "foo-1",
216→            //     "foo-2"
217→            // )) { ... }
218→            // but reject:
219→            // for (
220→            //     foo in listOf(
221→            //         "foo-1",
222→            //         "foo-2"
223→            //     )
224→            // ) { ... }
225→            return
226→        }
227→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
228→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
229→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
230→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
231→                    it.textLength > 0
232→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
233→            // IDEA quirk:
234→            // if (true &&
235→            //     true
236→            // ) {
237→            // }
238→            // instead of
239→            // if (
240→            //     true &&
241→            //     true
242→            // ) {
243→            // }
244→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
245→        ) {
246→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
247→        }
248→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
249→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
250→        }
251→    }

253→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
254→        node: ASTNode,
255→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
256→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
257→    ) {
258→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
259→        if (
260→            node.textContains('\n') &&
261→            entries.size > 1 &&
262→            // e.g.
263→            //
264→            // class A : B, C,
265→            //     D
266→            // or
267→            // class A : B, C({
268→            // }), D
269→            //
270→            // but not
271→            //
272→            // class A : B, C, D({
273→            // })
274→            !(
275→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
276→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
277→                )
278→        ) {
279→            // put space after :
280→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
281→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
282→                if (
283→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
284→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
285→                ) {
286→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
287→                }
288→            }
289→            // put entries on separate lines
290→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
291→            for (c in node.children()) {
292→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
293→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
294→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
295→                ) {
296→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
297→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
298→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
299→                        emit = emit,
300→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
301→                    )
302→                }
303→            }
304→        }
305→    }
306→
307→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
308→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
309→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
310→
311→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315→    ) {
316→        for (c in node.children()) {
317→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320→                else -> false
321→            }
322→            if (hasLineBreak) {
323→                // rearrange
324→                //
325→                // a, b, value(
326→                // ), c, d
327→                //
328→                // to
329→                //
330→                // a, b,
331→                // value(
332→                // ),
333→                // c, d
334→
335→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337→                if (
338→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→                ) {
341→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342→                }
343→                // insert \n after multi-line value
344→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347→                if (
348→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351→                    // value(
352→                    // ), // a comment
353→                    // c, d
354→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355→                ) {
356→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357→                }
358→            }
359→        }
360→    }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
363→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
364→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
365→
366→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
367→        node: ASTNode,
368→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
369→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370→    ) {
371→        node
372→            .treeParent
373→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
374→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
375→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
376→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
377→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
378→            ?.let {
379→                // rewriting
380→                // """
381→                //     text
382→                // _""".trimIndent()
383→                // to
384→                // """
385→                //     text
386→                // _
387→                // """.trimIndent()
388→                emit(
389→                    node.startOffset,
390→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
391→                    true,
392→                )
393→                if (autoCorrect) {
394→                    node as LeafPsiElement
395→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
396→                }
397→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
398→            }
399→    }
400→
401→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
402→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
403→        // if token is in lTokenSet
404→        //     find matching rToken
405→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
406→        // return false
407→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
408→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
409→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
410→            // special cases:
411→            // x = y.f({ z ->
412→            // })
413→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
414→            // 2, 3)
415→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
416→        }
417→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
418→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
419→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
420→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
421→        }
422→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
423→            return true
424→        }
425→        return false
426→    }
427→
428→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
429→        node: ASTNode,
430→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
431→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
432→    ) {
433→        val p = node.treeParent
434→        if (
435→            // check
436→            // `{ p -> ... }`
437→            // and
438→            // `when { m -> ... }`
439→            // only
440→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
441→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
442→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
443→            // permit
444→            // when {
445→            //     m -> 0 + d({
446→            //     })
447→            // }
448→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
449→            // permit
450→            // when (this) {
451→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
452→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
453→            //     -> true
454→            // }
455→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
456→        ) {
457→            return
458→        }
459→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
461→        }
462→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
463→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
464→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
465→        }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
469→        node: ASTNode,
470→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
471→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
472→        indent: String,
473→    ) {
474→        emit(
475→            node.startOffset - 1,
476→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
477→            true,
478→        )
479→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
480→        if (autoCorrect) {
481→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
486→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
487→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
488→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
489→        indent: String? = null,
490→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
491→    ) {
492→        emit(
493→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
494→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
495→            true,
496→        )
497→        LOGGER.trace {
498→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
499→        }
500→        if (autoCorrect) {
501→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
502→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
503→        }
504→    }
505→
506→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
507→        for (child in node.children()) {
508→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
509→                val v = child.text
510→                if (v == "\n") {
511→                    return true
512→                }
513→            }
514→        }
515→        return false
516→    }
517→
518→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
519→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
520→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
521→            textContains('\n')
522→        } else {
523→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
524→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
525→        }
526→    }
527→
528→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
529→
530→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
531→
532→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
533→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
534→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
535→
536→    /**
537→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
538→     *     for (foo in listOf(
539→     *         "foo-1",
540→     *         "foo-2"
541→     *     )) { ... }
542→     * but reject:
543→     *     for (
544→     *         foo in listOf(
545→     *             "foo-1",
546→     *             "foo-2"
547→     *         )
548→     *     ) { ... }
549→     */
550→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
551→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
555→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
556→        }
557→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
558→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
559→        }
560→
561→        var node: ASTNode? = this
562→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
563→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
564→                return false
565→            }
566→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
567→        }
568→        return true
569→    }
570→
571→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
572→        node: ASTNode,
573→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
574→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
575→    ) {
576→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
577→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
578→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
579→                return
580→            }
581→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
582→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
583→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
584→            val wrapBlock =
585→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
586→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
587→                    )
588→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
589→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
590→                    endOfBlock,
591→                    autoCorrect,
592→                    emit,
593→                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
594→                )
595→            }
596→        }
597→    }
598→
599→    private companion object {
600→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
601→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
602→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
603→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
604→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
605→            ).toMap()
606→    }
607→}
608→
609→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId

 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 28→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * Wrapping of context receiver to a separate line. Arguments of the context receiver are wrapped to separate line
 33→ * whenever the max line length is exceeded.
 34→ */
 35→public class ContextReceiverWrappingRule :
 36→    StandardRule(
 37→        id = "context-receiver-wrapping",
 38→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 39→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 40→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 41→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 42→        ),
 43→    ),
 44→    Rule.Experimental {
 45→    private lateinit var indent: String
 46→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        val indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 50→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 51→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 52→        )
 53→        indent = indentConfig.indent
 54→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 58→        node: ASTNode,
 59→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 60→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 61→    ) {
 62→        when {
 63→            node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ->
 64→                visitContextReceiverList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 65→            node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST && node.isPartOf(CONTEXT_RECEIVER) ->
 66→                visitContextReceiverTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    private fun visitContextReceiverList(
 71→        node: ASTNode,
 72→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 73→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 74→    ) {
 75→        // Context receiver must be followed by new line or comment
 76→        node
 77→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 78→            .nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 79→            ?.takeIf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 80→            ?.let { nodeAfterContextReceiver ->
 81→                emit(nodeAfterContextReceiver.startOffset, "Expected a newline after the context receiver", true)
 82→                if (autoCorrect) {
 83→                    nodeAfterContextReceiver
 84→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + node.treeParent.lineIndent())
 86→                }
 87→            }
 88→
 89→        // Check line length assuming that the context receiver is indented correctly. Wrapping rule must however run
 90→        // before indenting.
 91→        if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
 92→            node.lineIndent().length + node.textLength > maxLineLength
 93→        ) {
 94→            node
 95→                .children()
 96→                .filter { it.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER }
 97→                .forEach {
 98→                    emit(
 99→                        it.startOffset,
100→                        "Newline expected before context receiver as max line length is violated",
101→                        true,
102→                    )
103→                    if (autoCorrect) {
104→                        it
105→                            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
106→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent)
107→                    }
108→                }
109→            node
110→                .findChildByType(RPAR)
111→                ?.let { rpar ->
112→                    emit(
113→                        rpar.startOffset,
114→                        "Newline expected before closing parenthesis as max line length is violated",
115→                        true,
116→                    )
117→                    if (autoCorrect) {
118→                        rpar.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + node.lineIndent())
119→                    }
120→                }
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun visitContextReceiverTypeArgumentList(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        val contextReceiver = node.treeParent.text
130→        // Check line length assuming that the context receiver is indented correctly. Wrapping rule must however run
131→        // before indenting.
132→        if (!contextReceiver.contains('\n') &&
133→            node.lineIndent().length + contextReceiver.length > maxLineLength
134→        ) {
135→            node
136→                .children()
137→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
138→                .forEach {
139→                    emit(
140→                        it.startOffset,
141→                        "Newline expected before context receiver type projection as max line length is violated",
142→                        true,
143→                    )
144→                    if (autoCorrect) {
145→                        it
146→                            .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent)
147→                    }
148→                }
149→            node
150→                .findChildByType(GT)

 75→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 76→
 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    StandardRule(
 87→        id = "wrapping",
 88→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 89→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92→        ),
 93→    ) {
 94→    private var line = 1
 95→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        line = 1
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129→
130→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132→            return
133→        }
134→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137→        val wrapBlock =
138→            when {
139→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143→                }
144→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
145→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf {
146→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
147→                    }
148→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf {
149→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
150→                    }
151→                    val line =
152→                        startOfLine
153→                            ?.leaves()
154→                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
155→                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
156→                            .orEmpty()
157→                    line.length > maxLineLength
158→                }
159→                else -> false
160→            }
161→        if (wrapBlock) {
162→            startOfBlock
163→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
164→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
165→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
166→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
167→                        autoCorrect,
168→                        emit,
169→                    )
170→                }
171→        }
172→    }
173→
174→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
175→        node: ASTNode,
176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
178→    ) {
179→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
180→        var newlineInBetween = false
181→        var parameterListInBetween = false
182→        var numberOfArgs = 0
183→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
184→        // matching ), ] or }
185→        val r = node.nextSibling {
186→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
187→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
188→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
189→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
190→            if (isValueArgument) {
191→                numberOfArgs++
192→                firstArg = it
193→            }
194→            it.elementType == rElementType
195→        }!!
196→        if (
197→            !newlineInBetween ||
198→            // keep { p ->
199→            // }
200→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
201→            // keep ({
202→            // }) and (object : C {
203→            // })
204→            (
205→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
206→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
207→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
208→                )
209→        ) {
210→            return
211→        }
212→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
213→            // keep:
214→            // for (foo in listOf(
215→            //     "foo-1",
216→            //     "foo-2"
217→            // )) { ... }
218→            // but reject:
219→            // for (
220→            //     foo in listOf(
221→            //         "foo-1",
222→            //         "foo-2"
223→            //     )
224→            // ) { ... }
225→            return
226→        }
227→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
228→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
229→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
230→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
231→                    it.textLength > 0
232→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
233→            // IDEA quirk:
234→            // if (true &&
235→            //     true
236→            // ) {
237→            // }
238→            // instead of
239→            // if (
240→            //     true &&
241→            //     true
242→            // ) {
243→            // }
244→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
245→        ) {
246→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
247→        }
248→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
249→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
250→        }
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
254→        node: ASTNode,
255→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
256→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
257→    ) {
258→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
259→        if (
260→            node.textContains('\n') &&
261→            entries.size > 1 &&
262→            // e.g.
263→            //
264→            // class A : B, C,
265→            //     D
266→            // or
267→            // class A : B, C({
268→            // }), D
269→            //
270→            // but not
271→            //
272→            // class A : B, C, D({
273→            // })
274→            !(
275→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
276→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
277→                )
278→        ) {
279→            // put space after :
280→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
281→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
282→                if (
283→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
284→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
285→                ) {
286→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
287→                }
288→            }
289→            // put entries on separate lines
290→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
291→            for (c in node.children()) {
292→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
293→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
294→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
295→                ) {
296→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
297→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
298→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
299→                        emit = emit,
300→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
301→                    )
302→                }
303→            }
304→        }
305→    }
306→
307→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
308→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
309→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
310→
311→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315→    ) {
316→        for (c in node.children()) {
317→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320→                else -> false
321→            }
322→            if (hasLineBreak) {
323→                // rearrange
324→                //
325→                // a, b, value(
326→                // ), c, d
327→                //
328→                // to
329→                //
330→                // a, b,
331→                // value(
332→                // ),
333→                // c, d
334→
335→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337→                if (
338→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→                ) {
341→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342→                }
343→                // insert \n after multi-line value
344→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347→                if (
348→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351→                    // value(
352→                    // ), // a comment
353→                    // c, d
354→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355→                ) {
356→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357→                }
358→            }
359→        }
360→    }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
363→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
364→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
365→
366→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
367→        node: ASTNode,
368→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
369→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370→    ) {
371→        node
372→            .treeParent
373→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
374→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
375→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
376→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
377→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
378→            ?.let {
379→                // rewriting
380→                // """
381→                //     text
382→                // _""".trimIndent()
383→                // to
384→                // """
385→                //     text
386→                // _
387→                // """.trimIndent()
388→                emit(
389→                    node.startOffset,
390→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
391→                    true,
392→                )
393→                if (autoCorrect) {
394→                    node as LeafPsiElement
395→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
396→                }
397→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
398→            }
399→    }
400→
401→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
402→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
403→        // if token is in lTokenSet
404→        //     find matching rToken
405→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
406→        // return false
407→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
408→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
409→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
410→            // special cases:
411→            // x = y.f({ z ->
412→            // })
413→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
414→            // 2, 3)
415→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
416→        }
417→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
418→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
419→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
420→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
421→        }
422→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
423→            return true
424→        }
425→        return false
426→    }
427→
428→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
429→        node: ASTNode,
430→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
431→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
432→    ) {
433→        val p = node.treeParent
434→        if (
435→            // check
436→            // `{ p -> ... }`
437→            // and
438→            // `when { m -> ... }`
439→            // only
440→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
441→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
442→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
443→            // permit
444→            // when {
445→            //     m -> 0 + d({
446→            //     })
447→            // }
448→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
449→            // permit
450→            // when (this) {
451→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
452→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
453→            //     -> true
454→            // }
455→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
456→        ) {
457→            return
458→        }
459→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
461→        }
462→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
463→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
464→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
465→        }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
469→        node: ASTNode,
470→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
471→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
472→        indent: String,
473→    ) {
474→        emit(
475→            node.startOffset - 1,
476→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
477→            true,
478→        )
479→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
480→        if (autoCorrect) {
481→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
486→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
487→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
488→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
489→        indent: String? = null,
490→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
491→    ) {
492→        emit(
493→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
494→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
495→            true,
496→        )
497→        LOGGER.trace {
498→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
499→        }
500→        if (autoCorrect) {
501→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
502→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
503→        }
504→    }
505→
506→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
507→        for (child in node.children()) {
508→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
509→                val v = child.text
510→                if (v == "\n") {
511→                    return true
512→                }
513→            }
514→        }
515→        return false
516→    }
517→
518→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
519→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
520→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
521→            textContains('\n')
522→        } else {
523→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
524→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
525→        }
526→    }
527→
528→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
529→
530→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
531→
532→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
533→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
534→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
535→
536→    /**
537→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
538→     *     for (foo in listOf(
539→     *         "foo-1",
540→     *         "foo-2"
541→     *     )) { ... }
542→     * but reject:
543→     *     for (
544→     *         foo in listOf(
545→     *             "foo-1",
546→     *             "foo-2"
547→     *         )
548→     *     ) { ... }
549→     */
550→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
551→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
555→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
556→        }
557→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
558→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
559→        }
560→
561→        var node: ASTNode? = this
562→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
563→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
564→                return false
565→            }
566→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
567→        }
568→        return true
569→    }

571→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
572→        node: ASTNode,
573→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
574→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
575→    ) {
576→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
577→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
578→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
579→                return
580→            }
581→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
582→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
583→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
584→            val wrapBlock =
585→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
586→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
587→                    )
588→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
589→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
590→                    endOfBlock,
591→                    autoCorrect,
592→                    emit,
593→                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
594→                )
595→            }
596→        }
597→    }

601→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
602→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
603→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
604→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
605→            ).toMap()
606→    }
607→}
